paragraphs: You have a Roman toe form with very narrow forefoot and a very wide heel. A mixed profile; forefoot and heel require different fits.

Beyond the obvious: Given your Roman toes, rather avoid very pointy shoes. These might squeeze your toe tips, even if the width of the shoe fits you. Given your particularly long arch, the ball of your foot may be pushed into the toe box. A squeezed forefoot may be caused by this instead of actual shoe width, so look for rather short toe boxes. Additionally your instep is particularly low, so an adjustable closure is preferable to avoid dead space. Ideally double velcro or laces, rather avoid pure slippers. Your shallow heel doesn't project as far back as most. Deeply sculpted cups will feel empty at the back. Your big toe drifts slightly inwards (could be a mild hallux valgus), hence we recommend a slightly wider and not too asymmetric shape.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: Your Tenaya Tarifa (EU 38, 3.5 down) is very aggressive for Tenaya, where the usual downsize is 1.5 sizes.
Your Tenaya Tarifa's squeezed toes is most likely a sizing problem, you could try a size larger for a better fit.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Roman toe form, narrow forefoot width, and wide heel width.

Given your preference for trad and multipitch outdoors on limestone and a comfort fit, we prioritize very stiff lace-ups with flat profile and no asymmetry, with ankle protection for trad/multipitch wear.
Note: data on shoe heel depth is unfortunately not widely available. We're working on extracting this data from shoe scans, but for now the recommendations match heel width only.
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Lace-up with Roman toe form, medium fit throughout. 4.5mm FS-Quattro rubber with a full, hard midsole, resulting in a firm shoe with flat profile and symmetric profile.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly wider in the forefoot and slightly tighter in the heel than we target.
